generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point clouds captured by 3D LIDAR sensor mounted on the vehicle chassis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance CameraSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard color cameras capturing raw images for object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraImageData
]

instance VehicleTelemetrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle state telemetry sensor providing speed, battery level, motor and steering status."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleTelemetryData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction – Data transmitted
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance CameraImageData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CameraSensor
]

instance VehicleTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleTelemetrySensor
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance ProjectedPathData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleTelemetrySensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARPointCloud : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw LiDAR point clouds before processing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance CameraImage : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw camera images for subsequent object tracking."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CameraImageData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleTelemetry : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Telemetry data comprising speed, battery level, motor, and steering status."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, ComputationOffloadingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ObjectTrack : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Object tracks derived from onboard LiDAR data for proximity monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance ProjectedPath : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Projected driving paths generated by the ACC planning pipeline."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedPathData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataMonitoringEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processes raw LiDAR point clouds to cluster and track objects."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Serializes data, aligns message types, and performs unit conversions per ISO 23247."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance DataMonitoringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Handles real-time data ingestion and feeds to the Data Monitoring service via ROS2."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService
]

instance ComputationOffloadingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC sensor-fusion and speed-planning computation to cloud or edge resources."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance RemoteManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Manages remote ACC control commands over WiFi with UDP."
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "UI streams real‑time vehicle state data, displays projected driving path, overlays detected object tracks, and supports data export, report generation, and temporal playback."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateInsight, DrivingPathInsight, ObjectTrackInsight, ExportDataAction, GenerateReportAction, TemporalPlaybackAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Hosts ACC calibration pipeline offloads sensor‑fusion and speed‑planning computation computed desired speeds are returned to vehicle actuator controller."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeDesiredSpeedAction
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows operators to remotely enable/disable ACC, adjust desired speed setpoints, and issue emergency braking commands."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction, DisableACCAction, AdjustDesiredSpeedSetpointAction, IssueEmergencyBrakingCommandAction
]

instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Processes point cloud to cluster and track objects, publishing track data."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackInsight
]

// C17: Insights
instance VehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Displays current vehicle speed, battery level, motor status, and steering pose."
]

instance DrivingPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shows projected driving paths calculated by the ACC system."
]

instance ObjectTrackInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Overlays detected object tracks within proximity onto the visualization."
]

// C17: Actions
instance ExportDataAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Exports logged vehicle and tracking data from the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates analytical reports from logged data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance TemporalPlaybackAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Plays back historical data for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ComputeDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Computes desired speeds based on ACC. Calculation performed off‑board and returned to vehicle control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remotely enables the Adaptive Cruise Control system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DisableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remotely disables the Adaptive Cruise Control system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance AdjustDesiredSpeedSetpointAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Adjusts the desired speed setpoint for ACC."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance IssueEmergencyBrakingCommandAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Issues an emergency braking command through the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands originate in the Digital Twin services where the Actuation FE packages steering, motor and servo commands (e.g., actuator setpoints expressed in standardized units). These commands are published as ROS 2 messages, serialized by the Data Translation component, and transmitted over the WiFi UDP network to the vehicle’s physical twin interfaces. The Actuation FE on the vehicle side receives these messages and forwards them to the respective actuator drivers. The current document does not specify authentication, validation, conflict resolution or external feedback mechanisms beyond the basic ROS 2 message flow or confirmation of actuator execution."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The system requires that all connected devices share a common notion of time with a synchronization accuracy of 100 ms or tighter, as mandated by safety–critical vehicular standards; this clock alignment is essential for computation offloading and coordinated operation across the Digital Twin and its physical twin. This 100 ms accuracy directly informs the maximum allowable latency in time‑sensitive services and sets the temporal granularity for data acquisition, processing, and actuation events."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin is architected using the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, partitioned into three entities: the User Entity, the Digital Twin Entity, and the Physical Twin Entity. Each functional element (FE) is mapped to an individual ROS 2 package, containing one or more ROS 2 nodes that encapsulate the FE’s behavior. The User Interface FE is decomposed into a 3D Visualization FE and a Control Panel FE. The Digital Twin Entity hosts data‑management and control mechanisms, including data storage, while the Physical Twin Entity represents the inherent resources of the vehicle (compute, sensors, actuators) and implements general automotive functions such as point‑cloud clustering and tracking. This entity‑based, component‑to‑host mapping allows each FE to be deployed on distinct hardware resources, thereby supporting decentralized execution and aligning with the ISO 23247 structure."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin employs a ROS 2 middleware stack for intra‑service communication, with message topics published and subscribed over the ROS 2 transport. For communication between the Digital Twin platform and the vehicle’s physical twin, it uses WiFi LANs employing the UDP transport protocol. The network topology follows a node‑based publisher‑subscriber pattern inherent to ROS 2, with no explicit mention of higher‑layer routing, bandwidth constraints, latency targets, redundancy or security protocols such as TLS or certificate‑based authentication in the provided document."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "Complies with ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing (Parts 1–4), particularly Reference Architecture Part 2 which defines an entity‑based reference architecture comprising Data Collection and Device Control, Digital Twin, and User entities made up of Functional Entities. The standard explicitly states that FE implementation is open; an example of FE building blocks is OPC Unified Architecture, indicating compatibility with OPC UA protocols for data acquisition, device control, and interoperability. No additional certification or compliance framework is specified in the document."
]
